content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I created this piece for the purposes of entering the AWS x Vercel H0 Hackathon. #H0Hackathon
I built Cast Wide as a simple idea at first. One input, and the tool rewrites it for Twitter, LinkedIn, and Reddit.
But while building it, I realized something I had not paid attention to before. Writing is not just about clarity. It is also about where it is being read.
A LinkedIn post and a Reddit post can talk about the same idea, but they need completely different styles. One needs structure and polish. The other needs context and a more casual tone. Twitter needs to be short and direct. The content is the same, but the delivery changes everything.
That was the main thing I wanted Cast Wide to solve. Not just rewriting text, but adapting it to the way each platform actually works.
While building it, I also learned that small changes in prompt design make a big difference in output quality. If you mix platform instructions together, the responses start to feel the same. When you separate them properly, the outputs become much more distinct and useful.
Cast Wide is still simple. One idea goes in, three platform-native posts come out. But it made me think more carefully about how much context changes communication.
This project was built solo for the AWS x Vercel H0 Hackathon. #H0Hackathon
